title: Fuel charge forms - Canada.ca
content:
## Removal of Fuel Charge
On March 12, 2026, Bill C-4, Making Life More Affordable for Canadians Act , received royal assent, formalizing the removal of the federal fuel charge. As a result:
- The fuel charge no longer needs to be reported or paid on all types of fuel and combustible waste in listed provinces (Alberta, Manitoba, New Brunswick, Newfoundland and Labrador, Nova Scotia, Nunavut, Ontario, Prince Edward Island, Saskatchewan, and Yukon) in respect of activities after March 31, 2025
- Registrants must file any outstanding returns and pay any amounts owing for reporting periods prior to April 1, 2025
- Affected non-registrants are still required to report and pay any amounts owing for reporting periods prior to April 1, 2025
This change impacts the Canada Carbon Rebate for individuals and small businesses , the Farmers Tax Credit, and Canada’s approach to pricing carbon pollution .

## Fuel charge exemption certificates – Did you know?
- A person receiving a delivery of fuel must provide the appropriate completed and signed fuel charge exemption certificate form at the time of delivery or before receiving the fuel
- The registered distributor that delivers the fuel must retain the exemption certificate and confirm to the person that the delivery is subject to the exemption certificate
Failure to meet these conditions will result in the application of the fuel charge. More information is available in the fuel charge notices .
- B400 Fuel Charge Return – Registrant
- B400-1 Fuel Charge Return Schedule – Registered Distributor
